name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Do not buy if you plan on using a steering wheel, at least for now. Wheel support is nearly non-existent and it feels like if an arcade racer tried to make it feel realistic but over tuned all the values. However, the controller feels fine. 
*edit* 
After the most recent update with physics changes wheel drivability is much better and more fun, however still not on the level of games like carX, Asetto or Forza this is a good change, but there are still other issues, here's a list.
1. very little to do in the game 
2. navigation is annoying without waypoints or better GPS
3. cars are locked to pre-customized body kits and all tires are super stretched
4. input mapping is a chore and very limited  
5. car Ai is just annoying and ruins driving on the open road with how narrow the roads are 
6. the car damage system only acts as an annoyance towards players and serves no point except to take players money and extend the already grindy progression

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
OK this might be long but ive been playing this game for a week an half on a controller and g29 wheel OK so these are some things that can be tweak.

1.Add full controller and wheel support plus accessories (hand brake).
2.physic for controller isn't sync well.
3.Add feature to adjust caster on fully upgraded cars. 
4.physic for tires can be seriously adjusted when on controller and gaming wheel the turning registration only register when driving super slow. 
5.adjust smoothness for head turning in cockpit camera setting.
6.add feature that physically/visually changes tire compound when upgrading tires. 
7.add feature to open hood/car doors/and trunks.
8.add feature to create custom driver for in car only.
9.add feature to save tunes.
10.the most important feature of them all add clutch with manual transmission. 
11.fix vinyl for front and rear bumper vinyl will not show.
12.add window tint vinyl.
13.add feature for upgrading transmission (example 3 gears,4 gears,5 gears) street sport pro racing transmission.
14.add feature that doubles drift points when in or hitting clipping points.
15.add feature to change cockpit features like steering wheel gauges rear view mirror shifter hand brake driver seat tow hooks basic jdm /drift things 

im pretty sure im missing some small things but i know the community will comment what they are so please add these features to the next update.

name: OverDrift Festival
review: When the game was small and unpopular, it was great. Now that it's famous, developers saw the opportunity to put as much microtransactions in the game as possible. 50% of the game is locked behing microtransactions actually. Initally you were able to obtain every car simply just by grinding. Now you can buy like 8 free cars and the rest you have to buy with DLCs and THEN grind for them and buy them with the in-game currency. If the developers don't A) lower the prices of the DLCs, because they are ridiculously expensive (you can get a whole game for the price of some of them) or B) remove them entirely and let players enjoy playing a game they paid not so small money for, players are definitelly going to quit playing it, like I am now. Maybe if they didn't give away tens of steam codes for free, they wouldn't need to scrub as much money off of the players as possible.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Absolute crap. That is my simplified overall view of this game. It is most definitely not worth the money they are asking for, especially for the additional DLC. For reference, I use a controller with my driving games. I do not use a steering wheel, so I cannot comment on how it is when using one in this game.

Pros:
- Well, it looks good for the most part. But, looking good can only get you so far.
- That's pretty much it.

Cons:
- There are some obvious spelling issues.
- There is no mouse support. So, when it first starts up, you're going to instinctively move the mouse and see about clicking on the whatever servers are available at the time to find that doesn't work. You have to use the arrow keys on your keyboard. This is the first game I've ever encountered that didn't have basic keyboard AND MOUSE support. Why the developers thought this was a smart idea is beyond me, as it makes absolutely no sense whatsoever.
- For me, the game started up in a small window; I'd say it was about 800 x 600. Which made seeing it on my 65" 4k TV difficult to figure out what I was supposed to do to start an actual game (which only showed 3 "sessions/servers" to choose from). I had to hit Ctrl + Enter to force it into full-screen just so I could continue.
- No "main menu" from the start to setup the game to your liking. No, common sense and basic human intelligence were not considered when it came to making this game. You do not gain access to the menu system until after you've picked a session to join.
- The overall main menu is a clusterf**k and looks sloppy/busy.
- The Graphics Settings menu doesn't actually do a proper full-screen ability. You can choose different resolutions to try and make it fill the entire screen, but it's not an actual full-screen. It's more like full windowed mode.
- I could not enable V-sync. It was an option, but it was an option that you could not enable.
- Control mapping is defaulted to the keyboard. This is almost always a sign that the people responsible for this genuinely do not know what they're doing when it comes to making a game. A DRIVING game. With keyboard mapping as default. A DRIVING. GAME. When the usual thing you'd use is a controller or a steering wheel. And, the control mapping menu doesn't even have the ability to give a selection of whether you want to use a keyboard, a controller, or a steering wheel. You can only manually edit each function individually.
- It appears that you cannot freely rotate the camera around. Why? Every other driving game out there can. It's pretty much standard practice. Oh right, I forgot. That whole "common sense and basic human intelligence" bit I spoke about earlier.
- When driving, my car wanted to just veer to the right on its own, with no input from me. Try to get it back inline and it'll just repeat the process. Naturally, steering the car and trying to keep it behaving normally wasn't going to happen. So, there's something going on with this game and having issues with tracking. It seems this is a bit of a known problem on the discussion boards.

That's pretty much it for pros and cons. I haven't put much time into this game because I didn't want to continue. When a game starts up and immediately comes across as a badly put-together attempt with no logical line of thinking involved, that tells me that it is not worth my time to mess with it anymore. A user shouldn't have to trudge through a sea of bulls**t to try and get the game to their liking before they even turn the wheel. There should be common practices already in place to make the first impression a welcoming one. Even the discussion threads show that the developer doesn't really bother addressing issues. And, a few users have commented about how this is another game that is going to die out real soon. I can see why.

OverDrift Festival is what happens when you get people who don't understand what the words "organized" and "thought-out" mean. Poorly laid out menu system, nonsensical mapping selection system, and broken features make this game Early-Access, in my opinion. It's not polished at all.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
T500RS review:

Mixed feelings for this one. Its a fun game with great gameplay. But its very arcady and the force feedback is not that good. Let me explain:

The autosteering is off, its hard to explain but ill try. The car straightens too much because the autosteering is countersteering too much so you constantly have to "steer" into the corner to stay drifting. (Im not talking about assists or anything like that, if you know you know...) And another thing is that it feels like the autosteer starts to slow during a transition and then the turning speed picks up at the end. (Ive got around 2000h in sim driving, my FFB settings are not the issue).

The rear is not really controlled with throttle but rather using the steeringwheel and the weight transfer is almost nonexistent.
Its hard to explain but its very off from RL. This is not a realistic sim but its playable but it takes some getting used to, car upgrades and some extreme tuning makes it OK. 

Even tho it has issues with FFB i really like this game, if you are thinking of buying it just be aware of this if you are playing with a wheel. 

What makes me like this game is the community and the gameplay. People are really nice and helpful. The maps are amazing. If they just tune the force feedback a little bit this will be a really good game!

As of right now its playable and fun and if they improve the FFB i will update this review.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Honestly a really fun game, i love the fact its open world and u can drive/teleport anywhere on the map within seconds, the graphics is great, and the car packs offers a lot of content for what u pay for, although im usualy against pay to win type of packs. Also a huge bonus that the game gets updated quite often and the developers genuinely seem to care to update the game and listen to the community wich is great!

However there is a few major cons about this game i'd like to see fixed/improved in the future. For starters there is massive server desync when u try to tandem with other people, Second of all no body modifications? and third of all when ur making liveries there should be an autosave/manual save feature on unfinished projects so that u don't have to do it all in 1 go. Also no way of sharing liveries with friends wich is sad. And also for cinematic/photo settings it lacks on some features compared to games like CarX and Forza Horizon 4/5.
Also there is no mods for the game wich is kinda sad, that's what makes games like CarX and Assetto Corsa so great so would love to see that in the future.

It will be probably take a while before this game can be compared with something like CarX/Assetto Corsa but im hoping it'll be better with time as it is extremly fun to play

Other than that i can definately recommend picking up the game.

Overall rating of the game : 8/10

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Definitely the best drifting game I've played in a while. 
-Selection of cars is amazing
-Customisation is amazing
-Physics feel amazing
-Game looks amazing. 

The only two things i'd change with the game right now are :
-The servers have pretty bad lag compensation (for me being an NA player, it has a couple seconds of desync.)
-The handbrake needs a bit more tweaking as it feels pretty weak right now.

The best part of the game surely has to be the Tandem lead/chase feature. For this price i say you have no reason to not buy it. The livery editor is also pretty nice to work with.  

I give it a solid 8.5/10 as of right now, could easily be a 10 with more updates.

BUT

Progression to earn cars is just way too few and far between, and you get random unlocks instead of being able to buy a car with in game currency, youre basically forced to wade through countless cars just to get the few you want, unless you just spend copious amounts of money, i bought the overpass hoping to have an easier time with progressing and earning money, but it barely helps and the overpass cars all have to be unlocked in the same tedious fashion.

If you guys just made this game what it is ( Heavily Forza Horizon influenced) and allowed us to buy cars in what order we want from an auto dealership of sorts, id def put way more hours into it

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
I could say that this game is CarX competitor, but I will not. I could say this game is cheaper than Asseto Corsa but I will not. Why? Because my mama told me that lying is a bad bad thing.

This game is grindy, expensive and you get more for less money in the games stated above. I have 8 hours in the game and I already told myself I don't want another game where I'll feel like a slave and grind those $$ just to drive with the car which I bought with real money. Yep, you heard that right. Most of the cars are paywalled. You get couple of cars (not a single one more than mid-low tier). And the system is total mess. Budget per car is the most stupid thing i ever witness in "sim" games which this one is not. Game  is arcade, feels like arcade and plays like arcade.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
A really cool drifting game just for the fun of it.
The community seems to be very friendly. You either encounter people who don't communicate at all or do it in a friendly way. I have yet to encounter anyone who is rude or toxic.

The driving physics feel great. Or should I say drifting physics, since this is what the game is all about?

The customization is nice! You don't have to stick to premade liveries, you can create your very own one ingame.
Lots of decals, wheels, tires, exhausts, etc. to choose from. Sadly no wings though. I'd love to put the iconic Evo2 wing on my 190E.

Money is earned by drifting, either in events or just in freeride.

Why so many negative reviews? 
While the people do have a point about the dlc stuff and having some customization like neon and tire smoke behind a paywall, they are exaggerating badly by saying "almost all cars are behind paywall". That's not true. You can unlock many cars with just the base game. I bought the "overpass" and the car pack that included my dream car. That's all I'll ever need in the game.

The graphics are quite okay, definitely better than comparable drift games though.

The sound design isn't too great. The car sounds are low quality and the music... ugh... It's a matter of your own personal music taste, but I'm grateful for the ability to turn the music volume to 0%. All in game music is some kind of techno dubsteb gangsterrap combined.

It plays very well with a steering wheel and uses 900°, so no worries, no only 180° ratio like in other games.

What definitely needs to be added in my opinion is an option to make the camera automatically look in the direction your drifting in cokpit view. I play only in cockpit view and tandem drifting is basically a gamble because I can't see the other car or even the track. 
Or maybe even a VR option. That would be ideal for me.

All in all probably one of the best dedicated drifting games around!

🔎 Long Version Checklist ➜ [u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/groups/expectationchecklist/discussions/1/4763207146661766064/]Reveal[/url][/u]

[h1] 🕘          IMPRESSION:[/h1]
* Year 2022: Game released 
* 15 min later: Started playtest on Demo, sadly it is not what I expected
* Year 2024: A well-edited video review pulled me back in
* 2 hours earlier: Cannot find an informative review but "very positive" rating on Steam?
* 2 hours later: Uncover the facts behind the beautiful rating

[h1]  🔊         TESTER MUST KNOW:[/h1]
* We can create a server with custom setting
* Click and wait 5 sec to teleport in the map
* Damage Penalty = Losing drift ability 
* Free roam to earn style points & convert into cash
* Enter cash zone to earn style points money
* Earned money is tied to each car, cannot spend on another car
* Disqualify event when player gone too far away from the circuit
* Lack of community livery to download, limited to 1 livery for only specific car
* Downloaded free demo will not able to join server anymore

[h1] 💎 SAME GENRE COMPARISON:[/h1]
* No race menu, must fast travel to each location to start a game
* No track wall, no AI bot, no ghost car
* Complete multiple circuit mode to unlock more cars
* Completed circuit track will not mark as completed
* Car collision with online players when drifting
* Deep customization option on tuning
* Many visual customization moved to DLC
* All the beautiful cars moved to DLC
* List of repetitive challenges for Daily Task & Weekly Task
* No ping display in the server list

[h1]  ⚠️        RUMOR CHECKING:[/h1]
* It is a fun game to play ➜ Happen only if you love free roam with public players
* Huge desync between players & poor optimized ➜ Disagree
* UI is terrible ➜ Because there are too many buttons on the screen at once
* Car become undriftable when drifting is the only way to earn money ➜ True, when the money is tied to each car, and damaged car require money to repair. This idea makes the car unplayable.
